steps.title: Identify submariner troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your submariner deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner -n submariner --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review submariner configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant submariner configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner
kubectl get configmap -n submariner -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=submariner
```
The globalnet IP implementation fails to work sometimes, it needs investigation.

Logs: https://api.travis-ci.com/v3/job/327959427/log.txt

steps.title: Apply the fix for Globalnet intermitent failures
steps.description:
The way subctl works is that it will first look for the existing clusters that are registered with the Broker cluster and then try to allocate a non-overlapping globalCidr to the newly joining cluster. So, in your case, even though you ran the subctl join command sequentially, submariner pods on
```yaml
bash-4.2$ kubectl get clusters -n submariner-operator
NAME                      AGE
nmanos-cluster-a-tunnel   46m
nmanos-cluster-b-tunnel   46m

bash-4.2$ kubectl describe cluster nmanos-cluster-a-tunnel -n submariner-operator
Name:         nmanos-cluster-a-tunnel
Namespace:    submariner-operator
Labels:       <none>
Annotations:  <none>
API Version:  submariner.io/v1
Kind:         Cluster
Metadata:
  Creation Timestamp:  2020-05-06T07:27:16Z
  Generation:          1
  Resource Version:    27076
  Self Link:           /apis/submariner.io/v1/namespaces/submariner-operator/clusters/nmanos-clust
```

steps.title: Confirm Globalnet intermitent failures is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=submariner -n submariner --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n submariner --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The way subctl works is that it will first look for the existing clusters that are registered with the Broker cluster and then try to allocate a non-overlapping globalCidr to the newly joining cluster.
codeSnippets:
bash-4.2$ kubectl get clusters -n submariner-operator
NAME                      AGE

nmanos-cluster-b-tunnel   46m

bash-4.2$ kubectl describe cluster nmanos-cluster-a-tunnel -n submariner-operator

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with submariner installed or the issue environment reproducible.
